strategy.name: 1-Minute Options Scalper
strategy.instrument: 0DTE or next-expiry ATM options (CALL for long bias, PUT for short bias)
sources: Price momentum — 1-min candle close vs previous close
RSI filter — only trade between 35–65 (tighter band than crypto)
VWAP filter — price above VWAP favors CALLs, below favors PUTs
Volume confirmation — current bar volume must exceed 20-bar average

indicators.rsi_14: Momentum filter. Only trade when RSI is between 35–65. Tighter than crypto to avoid chop.
indicators.vwap: Directional bias. Price above VWAP = favor CALLs. Price below VWAP = favor PUTs.
indicators.volume: Confirmation filter. Only enter if 1-min bar volume is above the 20-bar average.
indicators.price_momentum: Compare last 1-min close vs previous close. Minimum 0.10% move required (stocks move less than crypto).
indicators.vix: Volatility regime filter. Check VIX level before every trade. Governs position size and trade permission — see vix_filter.

vix_filter.description: Check CBOE:VIX on TradingView before entering any trade. VIX level determines regime.

low.action: Trade normally — full size, standard rules apply
low.note: Low volatility = smaller option premiums, tighter moves. Scalps may be smaller.

normal.action: Trade normally — full size, standard rules apply

elevated.action: Reduce position size by 50%. Widen stop loss to -20% on premium.
elevated.note: More volatile — premiums expand but reversals are faster. Smaller size protects capital.

high.action: Max 1 trade at a time. Reduce size to 25% of normal. Only trade AMZN or MSFT (most liquid).
high.note: Market is in stress. Most scalp setups will fail. Be very selective.

extreme.note: Extreme fear/dislocation. Spreads blow out, fills are unreliable. No edge exists for scalping.

market_hours.avoid_open: Skip first 15 minutes (9:30–9:45 AM) — spreads are wide and price is erratic
market_hours.avoid_close: Stop trading at 3:30 PM — 0DTE theta decay accelerates sharply in final 30 min
market_hours.avoid_events: No trades within 30 minutes of scheduled earnings, FOMC, or CPI announcements

bullish_call: Last 1-min candle closed higher than the one before it by at least 0.10%
bearish_put: Last 1-min candle closed lower than the one before it by at least 0.10%
neutral_skip: Candle close change is less than 0.10% (chop — skip)
Price is within 0.05% of VWAP (indecision — skip)
Volume is below 20-bar average (low conviction — skip)
VIX is above 40 (extreme regime — no trades at all)

call: Buy ATM CALL — nearest expiry (0DTE preferred on SPY/QQQ, next weekly on single stocks)
Strike: at-the-money or one strike in-the-money
Enter at market on next 1-min candle open
put: Buy ATM PUT — nearest expiry (0DTE preferred on SPY/QQQ, next weekly on single stocks)
Strike: at-the-money or one strike in-the-money
Enter at market on next 1-min candle open

exit_rules: Profit target: exit when option premium gains +25% from entry price
Stop loss: exit when option premium loses -15% from entry price
Time stop: exit after 5 minutes regardless of P&L — do not hold a scalp longer than 5 bars
Hard close: exit ALL positions by 3:30 PM — never hold 0DTE into the final 30 minutes
risk_rules: Check VIX regime FIRST before placing any trade — see vix_filter for size and permission rules
Fixed risk per trade: 1% of account per option contract (scale down per vix_filter in elevated+ regimes)
Maximum premium per trade: $50 normal, $25 when VIX is elevated (20–30), $12 when VIX is high (30–40)
Maximum 3 open positions at one time (1 max when VIX is high)
Only trade AMZN or MSFT when VIX is above 30 — tightest spreads under stress
Do not trade during earnings week on single stocks
Paper trade first — validate signal accuracy before using real capital

symbol_guidance.MU: Semiconductor. Earnings-sensitive — check calendar before trading. Good options volume.
symbol_guidance.AMZN: Mega-cap. Tight spreads, reliable liquidity. Strong VWAP respector intraday.
symbol_guidance.PLTR: High retail interest, decent options volume. Can gap — watch size on volatile days.
symbol_guidance.SOFI: Smaller cap — expect wider bid/ask spreads. Use smaller size, confirm volume filter.
symbol_guidance.NFLX: High IV — premiums are expensive. Only enter on very strong momentum signals.
symbol_guidance.MSFT: Mega-cap. Tight spreads, consistent liquidity. Good for cleaner scalp setups.
symbol_guidance.APP: High momentum stock. Fast moves — honor the 5-minute time stop strictly.
symbol_guidance.AMD: Semiconductor. High options volume, strong intraday moves. Good scalping candidate.
symbol_guidance.VIX: Volatility index — DO NOT trade options on VIX directly. Use as a filter: VIX above 20 = wider stops allowed, VIX above 30 = reduce size or skip scalps entirely.

notes: Options scalping strategy for high-liquidity US equities. Uses 1-min momentum + RSI + VWAP + volume filters. Buys short-dated ATM calls or puts. Exits on profit target (+25%), stop (-15%), or 5-minute time stop. Avoid first/last 30 min of session. Paper trade to validate before going live.
